ideation_trigger.raw_prompt: Turn a one-line product idea into a board-approved PRD + Jira Epic.
ideation_trigger.verbatim: Turn a one-line product idea into a board-approved PRD + Epic in Jira. Board approval gate on exit.

extracted_signals.problem_statement: The path from a one-line product idea to a board-approved PRD is the single most expensive hand-off in any engineering org, and the hand-off that loses the most context. Today the idea travels through PM brain -> Slack thread -> ad-hoc doc -> Jira ticket -> team kickoff; the PRD drifts from the conversation, the conversation drifts from the PRD, and the eventual Jira epic references neither. There is no machine-readable intermediate between the idea and the PRD, no deterministic record of which inputs shaped which conclusions, and no audit trail a CISO will accept. Existing AI tools (code-review bots, pair programmers, PM-side copilots) do not close this gap because they see at most one link in the chain.
extracted_signals.primary_user_persona: Product Manager at a Series-B-Series-D SaaS company, 50-500 engineers. Drops a one-paragraph feature idea in a Slack channel or in the Forge console's 'New idea' form, expects a Draft PRD back in under 10 minutes, and is willing to refine the gaps but not willing to author the structure themselves.
secondary_user_personas: Engineering Lead - needs the Draft PRD to be the right shape for the Architect stage to pick up without rework
CTO / VP Eng - needs the Draft PRD to surface ambiguity explicitly so they can resolve it before Architect work burns budget; this is the persona that signs off on the stage gate
Customer CISO - needs every step the agent took to be auditable, including which Knowledge Layer files it read and which MCP calls it made
success_criteria: A user-supplied one-line prompt is normalized into a requirement_brief and a Draft PRD in a single agent run, with no human in the loop before the artifact is produced.
The Draft PRD passes a structural lint against workspace/project/PRD.md (every required section is present and non-empty; no section marked TBD without a justification).
The agent surfaces ambiguity as a structured list, not a hidden guess - every section marked 'partial' must have a paired open_question_id.
The full input trail (Knowledge Layer files read, MCP calls made, prompt/response pairs when FORA_LOG_LLM=1) is captured in the audit log under the run's run_id.
The agent never writes to the Knowledge Layer; humans remain the sole writers in v1.
Every success metric in section 8 of the Draft PRD has a number, baseline, and target.
constraints: Model-agnostic: must run on Anthropic Claude (primary) with a documented OpenAI fallback path.
Per-tenant isolation: a Forge Ideation run for tenant A must never read tenant B's Knowledge Layer or MCP data.
Token + dollar budget per run: target $0.50 median, hard ceiling $5.00 for sub-goal 1.1 (below the platform-wide $50 cap; the gap is intentional headroom for downstream stages).
Stage gate: the agent must not pass the Draft PRD to the next stage (Market & tech-debt analysis) until the board approval interaction resolves.

Knowledge Layer reads are restricted to the v1 allow-list: workspace/project/PRD.md, workspace/project/roadmap.md, workspace/project/tech-stack.md, workspace/customer/standards.md, workspace/customer/conventions.md, workspace/customer/glossary.md, workspace/memory/ideation.md.
MCP read-set for sub-goal 1.1 is Jira only (Q3 2026 milestone); Zendesk / Slack / Confluence are stubbed with not_wired responses and a fallback note.

ambiguity_flags.question: Is the trigger for this iteration a real customer one-line idea, or a self-bootstrapping dogfood where FORA is the product being specced? The Draft PRD scope flips on the answer.
MCP read-set for sub-goal 1.1: proceed with Jira-only and stub the others (Zendesk, Slack, Confluence), or wait for the priority-1 set to be wired in Q4 2026?
Should the Draft PRD include a 'first design partner scenario' worked example, or stay template-clean and push that to a separate example artifact?
Is the per-run $0.50 median / $5.00 ceiling measured in input+output tokens only, or does it also include the MCP tool-call cost envelope?
Owner reassignment for sub-goals 1.2-1.4: one-shot CEO action now (assign 1.2-1.4 to ba-agent at creation time so the gate auto-resumes them), or staged manually after each 1.x is done?

workspace_files_read.purpose: platform PRD template (the 11-section mirror target for the Draft PRD)
stage gates and milestones; Q3 2026 is the current quarter
what is and is not buildable; MCP priority-1 set; Q3 2026 ships Jira only
SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / NIST SSDF / OWASP / WCAG inheritance; the audit baseline
naming, repo layout, delivery norms; per-tenant override model
FORA-specific terms; the dictionary that lets a cold-started agent read the rest of the workspace

queries_run: project = FORA AND parent = FORA-17 ORDER BY created DESC
findings: FORA-59 (this issue) - Sub-goal 1.1, status: in_progress, assignee: ba-agent (Hire #3)
FORA-60 - Sub-goal 1.2 (Market & tech-debt analysis), status: blocked, dependent on FORA-59
FORA-61 - Sub-goal 1.3 (Epic + story generator), status: blocked, dependent on FORA-60
FORA-62 - Sub-goal 1.4 (Jira sync), status: blocked, dependent on FORA-61

zendesk.fallback: Knowledge Layer only; v1.1 wiring per roadmap.md section 5

slack.fallback: Knowledge Layer only; Slack/Teams MCP is a Q4 2026 milestone per roadmap.md section 4

confluence.fallback: Knowledge Layer only; Confluence MCP is a Q4 2026 milestone per roadmap.md section 4

epic_stub.title: TBD - gated on board approval of Draft PRD (Q1 trigger semantics + Q2 MCP read-set)
epic_stub.acceptance_criteria_summary: TBD - derived from the Draft PRD's success metrics and acceptance criteria once Q1 and Q2 resolve.

next_action.owner: board (via ask_user_questions interaction on FORA-59) -> ba-agent (on resume)
